summary: The Docker-based reproduction starts the vulnerable application and demonstrates that a JWT token forged with the hardcoded default secret 'claude-ui-dev-secret-change-in-production' is accepted by the authenticateToken middleware, granting access to protected API endpoints.
expected_security_impact: A JWT token forged using the hardcoded default secret should be accepted by the server's authenticateToken middleware, returning HTTP 200 on protected endpoints such as /api/auth/user.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction verified the hardcoded secret in server/middleware/auth.js line 6, forged a JWT token signed with this secret, and confirmed the server accepts the forged token on protected endpoints.
verification_method: The verify.sh script (1) confirms the hardcoded secret exists in the source code via grep, (2) installs dependencies and starts the server, (3) forges a JWT token using the hardcoded secret, and (4) sends an authenticated request to /api/auth/user expecting HTTP 200.
evidence_produced_by_run: Forged JWT token signed with the hardcoded secret is accepted by the server
The authenticateToken middleware verifies tokens using the same hardcoded secret at line 53
Tokens generated at auth.js:70-78 never expire (no expiresIn field)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
